<!-- Generated by the crown-jewel-deep-plan ultracode workflow (28 agents, ~2.8M tokens, ~31 min). 2026-06-23. -->

# Crown Jewel Plan: A GenMLX REPL-Driven ClojureScript Programmer
### Final, gated, adversarially-revised — the document to act on

---

## 1. Executive Summary

The Phase-3 numbers do **not** support the north-star thesis ("a verified REPL loop is the right interface"), and — confronting this head-on rather than deferring it — the strongest evidence already in hand points the *other* way: the 35B's loop (22%) is net-negative versus its own one-shot (33%), losing on 4 of the 5 families where they differ. A search operator that destroys a strong model's existing capability on 4/5 families is not a neutral instrument awaiting the right regime; it is **prima facie evidence that the strict-evidence ratchet over whole-program re-emission is an actively harmful search operator.** The code confirms the mechanism: `synth/step` accepts only on `> cur-ev + plateau-eps` (strict greedy ratchet, eps=0.05), `score-candidates` scores the emitted program at its emitted σ (~1), and the σ-refiner is a *separate* union arm that only fires *after* every obs site already carries a noise arg — so a structural edit is first scored in its deep fixed-σ valley (varying-slopes partial = −63.76 @σ=0.5 vs −21 @σ=3.0) and rejected before σ can rescue it.

But the thesis is also not yet *decisively* falsified, because the falsifying experiment has not been run cleanly: the metric unions a deterministic σ-grid floor into the "loop" credit (shared-mean 3/3 with **zero LLM**; only 7/27 eval tasks differ base-vs-SFT), and the honest competitor — **compute-matched best-of-K one-shot + exact-evidence reranking** — has never been measured as a parallel arm on the same curriculum. The deepest verified finding both reframes and bounds the fix: the exact oracle already exposes a strictly monotone partial-credit path for added latents *when noise is co-refined* (per-group-means k=0..4 = −45.4/−35.4/−30.4/−27.9/−27.9, crossing the bar at k=2) — so the multi-latent valley is a **search-operator + accept-ordering** problem, not a reward-shape or capacity wall. Student capacity is demoted to minor-and-confounded (the 35B fails `linear` in-loop too, byte-identically); RL/GRPO is not-a-cause (it sharpens what SFT covers; its eval is vacuous).

**The decision discipline this plan enforces:** the loop must earn the crown against the strategy the data already favors, not against a strawman. Every phase carries a permanent **one-shot-best-of-K-with-oracle-rerank** baseline arm, compute-matched, on the **natural-difficulty** curriculum, with the σ-grid floor *subtracted* and any σ-line-search *ablated out* — so a "win" can never be deterministic-search or extra-compute laundered as an LLM-loop win. **Do not proceed to Phase 4 yet.** Run a short, free-first, falsification-shaped thesis-test (R−1 offline re-score → R0 de-mask + baseline arm → R1 σ-ablated/soft-accept → R2 adversarial compute-matched bake-off, the latter including a *real* RB-scored GMM, not a toy enumerable one). The Jetson + Qwen3-Coder-Next is the right hardware bet, but its verified value is sharpest for the strategy the evidence favors — offline best-of-K **one-shot** gold harvest + rerank — and its spend is gated on R2. Critically, Phase 4 (the resource-rational metareasoner) **has a real problem to solve in *either* outcome**: allocating compute across {cheap small proposer, expensive code teacher, oracle, K-draws-before-rerank} is a genuine VOC problem that does *not* require the loop-beats-one-shot thesis to hold. The crown is preserved; only one indicted component (the greedy evidence-ratchet as a *search* engine) is on trial.

---

## 2. Ranked Root Causes (most fundamental first, verified severity)

| # | Root cause | Verified severity | Why it's at this rank | Owning evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **RC0** | **The greedy strict-evidence ratchet over whole-program re-emission is the wrong search operator for coordinated multi-latent structure** — and the 35B's loop being net-negative vs its own one-shot (−11 pts, 4 lock-ins : 1 gain) is direct evidence, not regime noise. | **Critical (newly promoted to root)** | This is the *operator-level* failure that RC1–RC3 are facets of. The honest null ("search-over-evidence is net-harmful for capable models here") is already measured. The plan must try to *falsify the operator*, not search for a curriculum where it survives. | 35B loop 22% < one-shot 33%; beam==greedy byte-identical on every structural family; `synth.cljs:363` strict ratchet |
| **RC1** | **Accept-rule × structure×σ ordering deadlock**: structural edits are scored at *emitted* σ (deep valley) instead of *grid-best* σ; the σ-refiner is a separate union arm that fires only *after* a structured spec is already accepted → the correct structure is rejected before σ can rescue it. | **Critical** | The concrete mechanism of the 0/9 cliff, confirmed in code (`harvest.cljs:46` refiner-gate; `synth.cljs:340` verbatim scoring). Fixable in ~30 lines — **but the fix risks laundering deterministic σ-search as an LLM win (see RC1-hazard).** | per-group monotone path; varying-slopes −63@0.5 vs −21@3.0; refiner deadlock |
| **RC2** | **Proposer never emits the coordinated structural move** on coupled families (varying-slopes 0/9 greedy AND beam for the 35B; base/SFT 0/3). The LLM contributes *zero* structure on linear/per-group/varying — byte-identical base==SFT==35B σ-grid floor. | **Critical** | The binding *capability* constraint. Search/RL provably can't touch it. **But raising one-shot quality (a stronger teacher) attacks one-shot, which already beats the loop** — so RC2's fix flows naturally into the rerank pivot, not the loop. | 7/27 eval tasks differ base-vs-SFT (all template/σ-grid); beam==greedy per-family |
| **RC3** | **Regime mismatch**: the curriculum has no band where the loop genuinely beats one-shot; every family is single-idea linear-Gaussian. **Treating this as "fix the regime until the loop wins" is curve-fitting the benchmark to the thesis.** | **Critical (re-scoped)** | Real, but the *response* must be adversarial: hold difficulty at the natural distribution and let the loop lose if it loses. Steepening is permitted only as a *secondary* probe, never as the GO gate. | varying-slopes byte-identical 0/3 all; 4 lock-in : 1 gain; 0 mixture/categorical in curriculum |
| **RC4** | **Shallow interface**: whole-program re-emission, not typed/localized/**reversible** edits. **Reversibility is on the critical path** — not "later debt" — because SMC/MCMC-over-construction (the textbook valley fix) *requires* reversible birth-death moves that form-level re-emission cannot provide. | **Major→Critical-for-the-fix** | RC4 is the mechanism through which the *most-likely-to-work* fix (soft-accept SMC) is enabled. The draft's "scoring doesn't need it" hides that "the operator that might save the thesis does." | `synth.cljs:340` scores `:code` verbatim; no inverse edits; R-soft needs birth-death |
| **RC5** | **Corpus quality (teacher-ceiling + truncation + net-harmful-loop)**: 56 rows, ~half template/σ-grid; 8/17 longest linear targets clipped at 896 (unbalanced programs trained in); and the harvested trajectories are from the 35B's *net-negative* loop. | **Major** | Distilling a teacher's loop that is worse than its own one-shot distills a process the teacher is hurt by. Below the ~10K "valley of code reasoning" floor. | `merged_train_rows.jsonl` 56 rows; truncation warnings; 35B loop net-negative |
| **RC6** | **Metric masking**: the σ-grid deterministic floor is unioned into the proposer and credited to "loop solve-rate." The reported numbers are not a proposer measurement. **And the proposed RC1 fix *welds* the σ-search into the accept rule, making the masking worse unless explicitly ablated.** | **Major** | Makes everything uninterpretable; must be fixed first AND the fix must not re-introduce it through the back door. | base==35B byte-identical shared-mean; `harvest.cljs:46` union arm |
| RC7 | **Student capacity (0.8B too small to compose)** | **Minor (confounded, and partly un-testable on this curriculum)** | Confounded by truncation + decode caps + flat-mandate; the 35B fails `linear` in-loop identically, so the family isn't loop-solvable by *anyone* → the worst-case R3 result is uninterpretable. The small model's *proven* niche is repair/templates (ar1 3/3 > 35B 0/3), not composition. | 35B linear in-loop 0/3 identical floor; LiveCodeBench wall exists but unshown-to-bite |
| RC8 | **RL/GRPO insufficient** | **Not-a-cause** | RLVR sharpens, can't teach; zero gradient where SFT never samples; eval vacuous (byte-identical, 70/70 transport errors). | `grpo_report.json` floored families; genmlx-boh1 weight-format gap |

**The reframe:** the standing story blamed greedy-can't-reach-structure (no monotone path) + corpus scale + 0.8B-too-small. Verification refutes the first (monotone path exists under σ-co-refinement), demotes the third (confounded + partly untestable here), and relocates the bottleneck to **RC0 (operator) + RC1 (ordering) + RC2 (proposer)** — with **RC4 (reversibility) promoted onto the critical path** because the fix most likely to work (soft-accept SMC birth-death) needs it. None is a fundamental capacity wall; the open question is whether the *loop operator itself* is salvageable or whether the crown is **reranking, not search**.

---

## 3. Should We Proceed to Phase 4 Now?

**No. Run R−1 → R2 first (~1 week, free-first). But note up front: Phase 4 survives *either* gate outcome — only its *substrate* changes.**

Reasoning, stated plainly and self-critically:

- **Phase 4 (genmlx.control) is a genuine VOC/metareasoning problem regardless of the loop verdict.** Even if the loop loses, Phase 4 allocates compute across {cheap small one-shot proposer, expensive teacher one-shot, oracle verifier, how-many-K-before-rerank} — a real metareasoner with a real *measured* cost gradient (0.8B ar1 3/3 at 46% cost > 35B 0/3). What changes is whether the control sites are `[:proposer]/[:stop]` over a *loop* or `[:tier]/[:K]` over a *rerank ensemble*. **The crown (resource-rational program synthesis as inference) is not contingent on the loop thesis.** This corrects the draft, which implied Phase 4 "collapses" on NO-GO — it does not; it re-points.

- **The decisive experiment is falsification-shaped, free-first, and most-decisive-first.** R−1 (offline re-score of *existing* on-disk trajectories under σ-co-refinement) needs **no model, zero GPU, hours** and decisively separates RC1 (ordering — re-scoring crosses the bar) from RC2 (proposer never emitted the candidate — nothing to re-score). This must run before any week of work.

- **The gate is adversarial and binary.** Phase 4-as-loop is justified **iff** R2 shows the loop beating **compute-matched one-shot-best-of-K-with-oracle-rerank** (CI-lo > 0) on the **natural-difficulty** curriculum, with the σ-grid floor subtracted and σ-line-search ablated, on ≥3 natural families — *not* ≥1 seed on 2 cherry-picked steep bands. If the loop cannot clear that bar even with soft-accept SMC + RB scoring on a real GMM, the honest crown is **best-of-K one-shot + exact-evidence rerank + bounded validity-repair**, and Phase 4 is built over *that* substrate.

- **The pivot is not a consolation prize — it is the strategy the evidence currently favors,** and it is measured as a first-class arm from R0 onward so the comparison is real, not retrospective.

**Proceed to Phase-4-as-loop iff R2's adversarial bake-off shows loop CI-lo > one-shot-rerank CI-lo on ≥3 natural families. Otherwise proceed to Phase-4-as-rerank-allocator. In neither case is Phase 4 abandoned.**

---

## 4. Small-Model Verdict

**Keep a small model as the fast tier — but its proven niche is *repair and templates*, not composition; the current 0.8B is a placeholder; and the "can it compose" question is largely *un-answerable on this curriculum* and must not consume three weeks of R3/R5 to re-confirm a confound.**

- **Do NOT drop the small tier (reject "Coder-Next alone").** The resource-rational thesis *requires* a genuine cost gradient. The 0.8B already *wins where the fast tier's frequency mass lives*: ar1 3/3 vs 35B 0/3 at ~46% per-call cost (14.2 vs 30.8 s/call). Coder-Next (~52 GB resident) is not cheaper per call than the 35B-A3B on common moves; the cheap tier is a real measured ~2× saving on high-frequency edits.

- **Locate the small tier's verified niche correctly: validity-repair + templates + σ-tuning, NOT composition.** The loop's one unambiguous win is weak-model *validity unlocking* (one-shot 4% → loop 26%) — that is a **repair** function (parse/schema/coverage fixes), not a **search** function. The 0.8B's only proven LLM-borne skill is templates (ar1). This is where to invest it.

- **The "0.8B can't compose" question is confounded *and partly untestable here.*** It was measured under three confounds (truncated targets; decode caps < 277-tok program; 2.3× flat-mandate). But even a clean re-run cannot settle it on this curriculum, because the families where it floors (`linear`, `per-group`) are **not loop-solvable by the 35B either** — so a floor result is uninterpretable. **Therefore demote the "clean compositional re-measurement" from a 3-week phase (old R3+R5) to a 1-day harness-hygiene fix folded into R0/R1** (raise caps 320→512 / 160→384, fix truncation, disable thinking). Do not run a dedicated compositional-capacity phase until R2 has produced a family the loop *can* solve — otherwise there is nothing to measure capacity against.

- **The verdict, gated on R2:**
  - **→ (A) keep the 0.8B as fast tier** if, *on a post-R2 loop-solvable family*, it emits complete, terminated, coverage-passing programs for that family's moves. This also proves the headline "a tiny model works."
  - **→ (B) middle 3–8B student** (most likely landing spot for *compositional* moves) if the 0.8B floors on a loop-solvable compositional family while the teacher solves it. The on-disk Qwen3-Coder-30B-A3B is the intermediate-teacher / fast-tier-ceiling candidate; the 0.8B narrows to the cheapest repair/σ-refine/template/route sub-rung. Capacity-gap literature warns 80B→0.8B is a large gap (SeqKD/on-policy dampen, don't erase; LiveCodeBench jumps 5× from 0.5B→3B).
  - **→ (C) drop the small tier** only if the clean small model owns no cost-adjusted frequency mass — already refuted for the template/repair share (ar1 3/3 > 35B 0/3).

**One line:** the small-model bet is alive and its niche is *repair + templates* (where it already beats the 35B on cost); the compositional-capacity question is confounded *and* untestable until R2 yields a loop-solvable family, so it is a 1-day hygiene fix now, not a 3-week phase.

---

## 5. Phased Roadmap to the Crown Jewel

Ordered cheapest-most-decisive-first, falsification-shaped. **Every LLM-arm phase carries a permanent compute-matched one-shot-best-of-K-with-oracle-rerank baseline arm** (the harness `synth_llm_probe.cljs` already has a one-shot arm — this is nearly free). Each phase has a GO/NO-GO gate.

---

### Phase R−1 — Offline re-score of existing trajectories under σ-co-refinement (hours, NO model, NO GPU) — *the literal first experiment*
**What it tests:** RC1 vs RC2, decisively and for free. Does re-scoring the *already-harvested* candidate specs at grid-best σ cross the bar?
**Steps:**
1. Load the existing trajectories from `~/genmlx-loop-artifacts/` (recorded `:spec'`/`:code` per candidate).
2. Replay them through a σ-co-refined `score-candidates` (reuse `harvest/noise-grid`, the 8-point grid).
3. Report, per structural family: does any *already-emitted* structural candidate, re-scored at grid-best σ, cross its bar?
**GO/NO-GO:**
- **RC1-confirmed** if existing candidates cross the bar under co-refined σ → the structure *was* proposed and the *ordering* killed it. Proceed to R0/R1.
- **RC2-confirmed** if there is nothing to re-score (the proposer never emitted the structural literal) → the accept rule is innocent; the proposer is the wall. **Skip R1's accept-rule work, go straight to the teacher question (R2/R4).**
**Cost:** hours. This separates the two critical root causes before a single GPU-second is spent.

---

### Phase R0 — De-mask the metric + add the baseline arm + cost the "free" oracle (1 day, no new model)
**What it tests:** can we see the real proposer signal, separated from the σ-grid floor — *and* is the oracle actually free?
**Steps:**
1. Report **LLM-only solve rate with the σ-grid floor subtracted**; re-score all existing runs (base/SFT/35B). Subtract shared-mean and any noise-only win from proposer credit.
2. **Stand up the permanent baseline arm:** one-shot-best-of-K-with-oracle-rerank, *compute-matched* (K·steps·seeds budget equalized to the loop), on the identical de-masked curriculum. This arm runs in *every* subsequent LLM phase.
3. **Instrument oracle wall-clock as a budget term.** Every candidate calls `msa_score`/exact/IS-fallback (`n-particles 2000`, `harvest.cljs`); at K=16 × steps=8 × revise=3 that is hundreds of oracle calls/task, 2000-particle IS each for non-conjugate families. Measure it. If IS-fallback dominates wall-clock on Thor CPU, that is a *pure-speed* argument for the RB-exact primitive (R2/R4.5) and it inverts the cost model the metareasoner optimizes.
**GO/NO-GO:** No NO-GO (instrumentation), but it reframes the headline and establishes the real contrast. The de-masked one-shot-rerank arm is now the number the loop must beat.

---

### Phase Day-0 Thor smoke (parallel with R0, blocking for R4) — verify the hardware assumption
**What it tests:** the plan's load-bearing assumption that mlx-lm runs on Thor CUDA *today*.
**Steps:** "Qwen3-Coder-Next generates one token via Python mlx-lm on Thor CUDA" — single-token smoke, before committing to R4 sequencing. Verify the oracle runs native-free on Thor CPU under nbb (it should, today, no CUDA port).
**GO/NO-GO:**
- **GO** → R4 sequencing stands.
- **NO-GO** (mlx-lm-on-CUDA is genmlx-ste5 open port work; MLX is Metal-first) → **named fallback now, not discovered at R4:** (a) harvest-on-Mac with the 35B (slower/weaker), or (b) vLLM/llama.cpp as the out-of-process teacher server on Thor. Do not let R4 block on an unverified CUDA assumption.

---

### Phase R1 — σ-ablated localized accept + plateau/soft-accept ablation (~2 days, no new model)
**What it tests:** RC1 *with the laundering hazard controlled* — is the cliff an ordering bug, and **how much of any fix is the LLM vs the deterministic σ line-search?**
**Steps:**
1. In `synth/score-candidates` + `check`, score each *structural* candidate at its grid-best σ (1-D inner optimization over the just-edited site's noise arg), **co-refining σ for ALL candidates including the incumbent on every step** (consistent maximization — avoids the empirical-Bayes over-acceptance bias of comparing a max-over-σ child against a fixed-σ incumbent).
2. **Mandatory ablation (controls RC6/RC1-hazard):** report every R1 result in two columns — (i) structural literal accepted at *fixed* σ, (ii) with σ-co-refinement. **A win that exists only in column (ii) is a deterministic-σ-search win, not an LLM-loop vindication, and must be labeled as such.**
3. **Over-acceptance guard test:** on data generated from the *simpler* model, σ-co-refinement must *not* accept a spurious extra latent. Ship this as a guard test (genmlx-iraj-adjacent honesty).
4. **Plateau-eps + soft-accept ablation (free, no model, belongs here not in R6):** sweep `plateau-eps` (default 0.05) and add a temperature-annealed soft accept `factor!(β·Δevidence)`. The varying-slopes staircase (+0.43 → … → +3.85) cannot survive a strict greedy ratchet if any intermediate dips; this is the cheapest test of whether soft-accept alone unsticks it.
**GO/NO-GO:**
- **GO** if a structural family moves off the byte-identical floor for any model **AND survives the σ-ablation (the win is not purely deterministic σ-search)** → RC1 confirmed, the LLM-borne gradient is real. Proceed to R2.
- **PARTIAL** if it moves only under column (ii) → the *deterministic refiner* climbs; the LLM still contributes only the discrete literal. This is information for the rerank pivot, not loop vindication.
- **NO-GO** → the obstacle is the proposer (RC2) or the operator (RC0); proceed to R2's adversarial bake-off.

---

### Phase R2 — Adversarial, compute-matched, RB-scored bake-off on the NATURAL curriculum — *the make-or-break Phase-4-as-loop decision gate*
**What it tests:** RC0/RC3 — does the loop beat **compute-matched one-shot + oracle-rerank** at the *naturally-arising* difficulty real users would write, *including the real cliff families the north star is actually about*? **This is inverted from the draft: it is a falsification test, not a search for a confirming steep band.**
**Steps:**
1. **Hold the curriculum at natural difficulty** (the distribution a real GenMLX user writes — the un-steepened families). Steepening (varying-slopes g∈{4,5,6}, per-group g∈{5,6,8}) is run only as a *secondary diagnostic*, **never as the GO gate**, and only after the (g, pp) band is *jointly validated* (sweep group-count and per-group sample-count together; 8 groups × 2 latents = 16 latents on 32 obs becomes under-determined at fixed pp=4 — raise pp with g; verify a stable bar and a genuinely monotone partial-credit path at each point, not from one probe config).
2. **Bring the *real* cliff families into the decisive test, not toys.** Wire RB-partial-scoring (`auto_analytical` eliminable part + low-variance IS residual) into `check` for a **real GMM mixture** and a **partial-pooling hierarchical band** — NOT just fixed-small-support enumeration. *The north-star bet stands or falls on these non-conjugate families;* a loop GO on linear-Gaussian families the strong model already one-shots does **not** validate the north star. (Promoted from R5+ aside to the decisive test — see RC4/Risk-4.)
3. **Test soft-accept SMC-over-construction here, before concluding the loop is net-negative.** beam==greedy byte-identical is a symptom of the *strict ratchet*, not proof search is dead; a particle population under soft accept can carry a temporarily-worse intermediate through the valley. This requires reversible birth-death edits (RC4) — implement the minimal typed `add-latent`/`remove-latent` inverse pair to enable it. **Do not kill the loop on the strength of the worst-possible operator (strict greedy) without trying the right one (SMC birth-death).**
4. Run `synth_llm_probe.cljs` at full budget (K=16, steps=8, seeds=5, max-tokens=512, revise=3) with the 35B (and, post-Day-0-GO, Coder-Next) across linreg/kalman/hier/vslope/**real-GMM**, three arms: **{strict-greedy loop, soft-accept-SMC loop, compute-matched one-shot+rerank}**, de-masked, σ-ablated.
5. **Held-out-FAMILY split (not just seed split).** Train/measure on 4 families, evaluate on 2 unseen families. The RRPS leakage precedent (`project_rrps_titleB_earned`: title-B win was leakage, did not survive test-split) is binding: a seed split of the same 6 parametric families is within-distribution memorization (the ar1-template trap). Report both seed-split and family-split numbers; the family-split is the headline.
**GO/NO-GO (the Phase-4-substrate decision):**
- **GO-LOOP** if **either loop variant** beats the **compute-matched one-shot+rerank** arm at CI-lo > 0 on **≥3 natural families including ≥1 non-conjugate cliff family (real GMM/hierarchical)**, on the **family-split** → the loop-advantage regime is real → Phase 4 is built over the loop substrate (R4-loop, R5, R6-loop).
- **GO-RERANK (the evidence-favored default)** if the loop ties or is net-negative against compute-matched one-shot+rerank even with soft-accept SMC + RB scoring → **the crown is reranking, not search.** Build Phase 4 over the rerank substrate (R4-rerank, R6-rerank). This is *not* abandonment — it saves the Jetson-months R5-loop-distillation would burn distilling a net-harmful loop policy.
- In **both** outcomes, the validity-repair loop survives as a *sub-component* (R6) — it is the loop's one proven win (weak-model one-shot 4%→26%), used for parse/schema/coverage repair, never for evidence search.

---

### Phase R3 — Harness-hygiene fix (folded into R0/R1, NOT a standalone phase) — small-model verdict deferred to a loop-solvable family
**What it tests:** nothing capacity-related yet — it removes the three confounds so any *future* small-model measurement is clean.
**Steps (1 day, already inside R0/R1):** raise eval max-tokens 320→512, GRPO max-completion 160→384, disable thinking; ship the Map/Unfold combinator rewrite + lift the flat-mandate so composition is ~50 tok not 277; fix corpus truncation (Thor seq≥2048, untruncated).
**GO/NO-GO:** none. **The A/B/C small-model verdict is NOT earnable on this curriculum** (the floor families aren't loop-solvable by anyone) and is explicitly deferred to "measure the clean small model on a *post-R2 loop-solvable* compositional family, or on the *repair/template* frequency mass where its win already lives." No "too small" conclusion is admissible before then.

---

### Phase R4 — Jetson + Qwen3-Coder-Next gold harvest at scale (~1 week, gated on R2 + Day-0)
**What it tests:** RC5/RC2 — does a stronger code teacher raise the per-family solve ceiling and produce *gold* (not net-harmful) trajectories? **The harvest target depends on the R2 outcome.**
**Steps:**
1. Stand up Coder-Next on Thor (42 GB disk / ~52 GB RAM, offline via Python mlx-lm CUDA — **teacher-only, never native**: Gated-DeltaNet + MoE-SIGTRAP blockers persist on Thor; oracle runs native-free on Thor CPU today).
2. **Harvest content is R2-gated, and this is the adversarial correction:**
   - **GO-LOOP →** harvest *gold loop trajectories* on the R2-validated loop-solvable curriculum, **solved-only / best-of-K-final filter** (never every sub-bar improving transition; never from a curriculum where the teacher's loop loses).
   - **GO-RERANK →** harvest Coder-Next's **one-shot best-of-K *solved programs*** (not loop trajectories). A code-specialized teacher's verified value is *one-shot quality*, which already beats the loop — harvesting its loop would distill a process it is hurt by. **Recognize that GO-RERANK harvest *is* committing to the pivot.**
3. **Mode B large-batch harvest** (~94 GB) on the *fixed/validated* R2 curriculum. Target low-thousands of *gold* rows (cross the ~10K "valley of code reasoning" floor; 56 → ~5K is a phase boundary, not an increment).
4. **Exploit the 128 GB for a parallel proposer population, not just a bigger teacher.** Hold N fast replicas (0.8B/3B) + 1 slow (Coder-Next) + the in-process oracle simultaneously (revive the banked `llm/particles.cljs` Route-A substrate). best-of-K across parallel temperature/seed/tier proposers *is* the SMC-over-construction substrate R6 wants — independent particles, not K serial samples from one worker. This is also the only way "the metareasoner allocates across three resources" becomes physically real rather than time-sliced.
5. **Teacher bake-off:** Coder-Next vs on-disk 30B-A3B vs 35B through the same oracle harvest (capacity-gap insurance; the 30B-A3B is the closer-teacher control for R5).
6. **Cross-machine repro discipline:** pin eval to ONE machine; checksum corpora; add a cross-machine oracle-determinism check (same observations → same exact evidence within the float32 floor on both Metal and CUDA — Metal/CUDA float32-aliasing differences must not move the marginal-likelihood numbers, or harvest/eval split numbers aren't comparable). Canonical corpus/checkpoint location pinned; public/private repo hygiene respected.
**GO/NO-GO:** GO if the chosen-mode harvest produces gold (not net-negative) rows at low-thousands scale and the teacher's per-family solve > 35B on the target families. NO-GO → the regime/method/operator, not the teacher, is the wall; re-open R1/R2.

---

### Phase R4.5 — RB-partial-scoring primitive, owned and gated (NOT an R5+ aside) — *the genuine technical core of the crown*
**What it tests:** Risk-4 / RC4 — the new primitive the north-star cliff families actually require. **Promoted onto the critical path because the crown cannot demonstrate its headline claim on the mixture/hierarchical families without it.**
**Steps:** build Rao-Blackwellized partial scoring = exact eliminable part (`auto_analytical`/`linear_gaussian`) + low-variance IS residual for the non-conjugate mixture/hierarchical structure (genmlx-iraj/71wh territory). This is the deepest unknown and the genuinely new capability.
**GO/NO-GO:** GO if RB scoring gives a *monotone partial-credit path* on a **real GMM and a partial-pooling hierarchical** model (the latter on a *low-per-group-n* band where partial pooling genuinely wins exact evidence — see Risk-6: do NOT abandon hierarchical on the well-separated-data probe alone; that conflates parameterization with capability). **If RB scoring gives no monotone path on these families, the loop thesis has no mechanism on the families it was designed for** — a hard scope cut the plan must state, not bury.
**Note:** R4.5 feeds R2 step 2 (the real-GMM bake-off) — so a minimal RB scorer is needed *before* R2's decisive run, with the full primitive hardened here.

---

### Phase R5 — On-policy distillation into the fast tier (~1–2 weeks, gated on **GO-LOOP** + R4 GO)
**What it tests:** can the fast tier learn *composition* (not just templates) from gold? **Skipped entirely under GO-RERANK** (no loop policy to distill).
**Steps:** SFT at seq≥2048 untruncated (Mode C, ~98 GB — the 248k-vocab OOM dissolves at 128 GB); then **on-policy step-wise distillation** (SOD/GKD reverse-KL, mode-seeking): student samples, teacher+oracle grade per-step, reweight by step-level divergence (the literature method for transferring agentic composition to sub-1B; forward-KL SFT on 56 truncated rows was always going to fail). **Mandatory closer-teacher control:** bake off the 80B→0.8B distillation against the 30B-A3B→0.8B distillation; do not ship the 80B-distilled 0.8B without the control (capacity-gap curse: student width caps transferable features). **Do not distill below the ~10K-row floor** — R4 must reach low-thousands of gold rows first (distillation *hurts* below floor; 56 rows was the worst zone).
**GO/NO-GO:** GO(A) if the student learns a *loop-solvable compositional family* (not just ar1) → keep the 0.8B as fast tier. GO(B) if it floors while the teacher solves → fast tier moves to 3–8B; 0.8B narrows to repair/template/route.

---

### Phase R6 — Phase 4 metareasoner (substrate per R2) + the structural-gain model it needs
**What it tests:** the crown jewel — resource-rational program synthesis as inference over the programmer-GF.
**Steps (common to both substrates):**
1. **Build the structural-gain predictor the VOC actually needs — this is the hard part of Phase 4 and the draft had no step for it.** A myopic one-step VOC with no learned value function defaults to "always cheap" (cheap-tier modeled gain is non-negative and free) or thrashes. So: **harvest a (state → which-tier-helped) dataset from the R4 dual-teacher / parallel-population runs** (both tiers run anyway), fit a cheap structural-gain predictor (logistic on state features: n-latents, last-Δevidence, plateau-depth, family-signature), and **gate the metareasoner on "the learned escalation policy beats always-cheap, always-slow, and round-robin on held-out tasks."** Without this, the metareasoner is undefined.
2. **Online co-resident loop (Mode A, ~80 GB):** the parallel proposer population from R4 (N fast 0.8B/3B + 1 slow Coder-Next + in-process oracle). Metareasoner = bounded one-step VOC over the control sites, reusing `genmlx.agents`. **Default cheap; escalate to slow only when the *learned* structural-gain predictor says modeled gain > cost.**
3. **Substrate-specific:**
   - **GO-LOOP →** control sites `[:proposer]/[:stop]`; soft accept `factor!(β·Δevidence)` + SMC-over-construction with **typed reversible birth-death edits** (RC4 — `add-latent`⁻¹=`remove-latent`); `genmlx.edit/CompositeEdit` at the construction→inference parameter-move boundary only. Validity-repair loop retained as a sub-component.
   - **GO-RERANK →** control sites `[:tier]/[:K]`; metareasoner allocates {cheap small one-shot, expensive teacher one-shot, how-many-K-before-oracle-rerank}; the loop survives *only* as bounded validity-repair on parse/schema/coverage failures (its one proven win), never as evidence search.
4. **Re-express the σ-grid refiner as a measured GFI move,** not a masked floor: a `ConstraintEdit`/`ArgsUpdateEdit` line search carrying a real GFI weight, so its contribution is *measured* in the cost accounting, not unioned invisibly.
5. **(Optional, last)** weight-format bridge (genmlx-boh1: 284 fused→split keys, prefix, A_log casing — a ~half-day deterministic remap) → GRPO as a *final pass@1 sharpener on already-covered families only*, never to acquire structure.
**GO/NO-GO:** GO if the metareasoner's *learned* allocation beats pure-fast, pure-slow, AND round-robin on **cost-adjusted, family-split** solve rate.

---

## 6. What Is Banked / Reusable from Phases 1–3

**Correct and load-bearing — keep as-is:**
- **The exact oracle** (`msa_score`/`exact`/`linear_gaussian`/`auto_analytical`/`enumerate`) — the free calibrated verifier (now *costed*, R0/R10: not literally free at K·steps·revise scale; IS-fallback is the speed risk). Native-free, CPU-portable to Thor *today*, no CUDA port. The same scalar serves training reward, loop accept, rerank ranking, and the metareasoner value function. **This is the heart and it works.**
- **The four-level check node** (`synth/check`: parse→schema→coverage→eval→fit, with delta-hack + coverage-hack guards). Correct; needs to become *incremental/differential* (re-score only the touched block) and σ-co-refined.
- **The native-free synthesis spine** (`synth.cljs`, `harvest.cljs`, `repl_corpus.cljs`, `curriculum.cljs` with calibration + well-posedness gating). Plumbing sound; the accept-rule, proposer, and reversibility layers change.
- **`genmlx.edit/CompositeEdit`** — fully built, reversible, GFI-weight-summing. Reusable at the construction→inference parameter-move boundary; the typed birth-death *structural* inverses (RC4) are new and on the critical path for soft-accept SMC.
- **The out-of-process worker pattern** (`scripts/llm_server.py` + sync curl) — the sync/async reconciliation that works; on Thor it runs ×N (parallel population, not just ×2).
- **`world/train.cljs`** — the clean GRPO membrane (with-trainer fence, KL-to-base, vocab-chunking). Correct; CUDA-port-gated and deprioritized to a final sharpener.
- **The banked `llm/particles.cljs` Route-A substrate** — the parallel proposer population the 128 GB unlocks; unused in Phase 3, central to R4/R6.

**Real but narrow wins:**
- **ar1 distilled skill** (SFT 3/3 > 35B 0/3) — proves a structural *template* can distill (not yet *composition*) and that the 0.8B owns the fast tier's template/repair share.
- **The weak-model validity unlock** (one-shot 4% → loop 26%) — the loop's one proven win, correctly re-scoped as a **repair** function (parse/schema/coverage), retained as a Phase-4 sub-component in both substrates.
- **The harness** (`synth_llm_probe.cljs`, 3 arms, seed replication, loop-vs-one-shot column) — reusable for R2 *after* adding the compute-matched rerank arm and the family-split.

**Banked as negative knowledge (proven dead ends — do not repeat):**
- More search/beam **under the strict ratchet** (beam==greedy byte-identical) — note this does NOT retire soft-accept SMC, which is untried and on the R2 critical path.
- More GRPO for missing structure (zero gradient where SFT doesn't cover).
- More rows from the **35B-loop on the same curriculum** (scales mediocrity; the 35B loop is net-harmful vs its own one-shot).
- Native LLM-as-GF as a prerequisite (Gated-DeltaNet + MoE-SIGTRAP; off the critical path).
- **Whole-program re-emission as the construction interface** — blocks reversible birth-death; replaced by typed localized edits in R2/R6.
- Forward-KL SFT at 56-row scale (below the distillation floor; SOD/on-policy required).

---

## 7. Top Risks

1. **R2 GO-RERANK is the likely outcome, and the team must accept it (highest-stakes, highest-likelihood).** The evidence already favors one-shot+rerank (35B one-shot 33% > all loops; beam==greedy dead; 4 lock-ins : 1 gain). The *risk* is curve-fitting the curriculum to force a GO-LOOP. *Mitigation:* the natural-difficulty + compute-matched + family-split + σ-ablated bake-off is designed to let the loop **lose if it's going to lose**, before a Jetson-month is spent distilling it. GO-RERANK is a *result*, not a failure: Phase 4 still has a real allocator problem, and the Jetson is *better* spent on offline one-shot gold harvest + rerank.

2. **R1's σ-co-refinement launders deterministic search into an apparent LLM win.** Welding the σ-grid line-search into the accept rule makes the masking *worse* unless ablated. *Mitigation:* the mandatory two-column report (fixed-σ vs co-refined) + the over-acceptance guard test. A win that exists only with co-refinement is labeled deterministic, not loop vindication.

3. **The mixture/hierarchical cliff — the families the north-star bet is *about* — could have no monotone mechanism even with RB scoring (deepest technical risk).** GMM and centered-hierarchical are non-conjugate. *Mitigation:* RB-partial-scoring (R4.5) is promoted onto the critical path and *into the R2 decision* (real GMM, not toy enumeration). If RB gives no monotone path on a real GMM and a *low-per-group-n partial-pooling* hierarchical band (the regime where pooling genuinely wins — Risk-6), the plan **states the scope cut up front** ("the crown excludes mixture/hierarchical"), which materially weakens the north-star claim — better said loudly than buried.

4. **Hierarchical was nearly abandoned on insufficient evidence.** The oracle prefers independent-groups for *well-separated* data — a property of that data regime, not of hierarchical modeling. *Mitigation:* generate a *low-per-group-n* partial-pooling band where hierarchical genuinely wins exact evidence before any abandonment decision (R4.5). Do not conflate parameterization with capability.

5. **Capacity-gap curse on the 0.8B (R5).** 80B→0.8B can underperform a closer teacher; student width caps transferable features. *Mitigation:* SeqKD + on-policy dampen it; the mandatory 30B-A3B closer-teacher control; the 3–8B fallback (verdict B). Never ship the 80B-distilled 0.8B without the control.

6. **Below the "valley of code reasoning" floor.** Distillation *hurts* below ~10K rows; 56 rows was 20× below. *Mitigation:* R4 must reach low-thousands of *gold* rows before R5; no distillation at 56-row scale.

7. **The metareasoner has no value function and defaults to always-cheap or thrashes.** A myopic VOC with no learned structural-gain model is undefined. *Mitigation:* R6 step 1 — harvest (state → which-tier-helped) from the R4 parallel-population runs, fit a structural-gain predictor, and gate R6 on it beating always-cheap/always-slow/round-robin on held-out tasks.

8. **Data leakage (the RRPS precedent is binding).** Seed-split of the same 6 parametric families is within-distribution memorization (the ar1-template trap that already produced a leakage-driven win once). *Mitigation:* held-out-*family* split (train 4, eval 2 unseen) as the headline metric throughout; report both splits.

9. **Day-0 Thor / mlx-lm-on-CUDA is unverified and the window is time-sensitive (~2026-06-27).** If mlx-lm doesn't run on Thor CUDA day-one (genmlx-ste5 open port work), R4 stalls. *Mitigation:* the Day-0 single-token smoke gate with a pre-named fallback (harvest-on-Mac-35B, or vLLM/llama.cpp out-of-process). No critical-path item is CUDA-port-gated; the oracle is native-free on Thor CPU today.

10. **The oracle is not actually free.** Hundreds of calls/task, 2000-particle IS for non-conjugate families, on Thor CPU. *Mitigation:* R0 instruments oracle wall-clock as a budget term; if IS dominates, that is a *pure-speed* argument strengthening R4.5's RB-exact priority and it must be reflected in the metareasoner's cost model (or the metareasoner optimizes the wrong objective).

11. **Cross-machine (Metal↔CUDA) float32-aliasing breaks reproducibility.** Harvest on Thor, eval where? If exact evidence differs across machines, harvest/eval-split numbers aren't comparable. *Mitigation:* pin eval to one machine; checksum corpora; cross-machine oracle-determinism check within the float32 floor (R4 step 6).

12. **Steepened-band under-determination.** Higher group counts at fixed per-group n make the regression under-determined (8 groups × 2 latents on 32 obs), shrinking the gold model's evidence advantage and exploding IS variance — the "+10-nat-per-step" claim is from one probe config. *Mitigation:* jointly sweep (g, pp), raise pp with g, validate a stable bar AND a monotone path at each point before any steepened run — and remember steepening is a *diagnostic*, never the R2 GO gate.

---

**Bottom line:** The plumbing is banked and correct; the bottleneck is precisely located at RC0 (the greedy evidence-ratchet *as a search operator*) + RC1 (ordering) + RC2 (proposer), with RC4 (reversibility) promoted onto the critical path because the fix most likely to work (soft-accept SMC birth-death) needs it. The decisive experiment is **falsification-shaped and free-first**: R−1 (hours, no model) separates ordering from proposer; R0–R2 (~1 week) run the loop against **compute-matched one-shot+rerank on the natural curriculum, family-split, σ-ablated, with RB scoring on a real GMM** — and let the loop lose if it's going to lose. If R2 produces a verified loop win on a real cliff family, the Jetson + Coder-Next + on-policy distillation + loop-substrate metareasoner is a coherent gated path to the crown. If it does not — the evidence-favored default — the crown is **best-of-K one-shot + exact-evidence rerank + bounded validity-repair**, the metareasoner allocates across {fast small, slow teacher, oracle, K}, the Jetson is spent on offline gold one-shot harvest, and the loop survives only as the repair function it provably is. **Phase 4 has a real problem to solve either way; only its substrate is on trial. Spend the free R−1 and the ~1-week R0–R2 before any GPU-month is committed.**

Key seams: `/Users/robert/code/genmlx/src/genmlx/world/synth.cljs:335-371` (accept gate / strict ratchet — R−1/R0/R1), `/Users/robert/code/genmlx/src/genmlx/world/harvest.cljs:37-79` (σ-grid + union refiner deadlock — R−1/R1), `/Users/robert/code/genmlx/src/genmlx/world/curriculum.cljs:298-334,558-587` (natural curriculum + macro-move; steepen as diagnostic only — R2), `/Users/robert/code/genmlx/scripts/synth_llm_probe.cljs` (add compute-matched rerank arm + family-split — R2), `/Users/robert/code/genmlx/src/genmlx/llm/msa_score.cljs:192-224` + `/Users/robert/code/genmlx/src/genmlx/inference/enumerate.cljs` + `auto_analytical`/`linear_gaussian` (oracle + RB partial scoring — R4.5/R2), `/Users/robert/code/genmlx/src/genmlx/llm/particles.cljs` (parallel proposer population — R4/R6), `/Users/robert/code/genmlx/scripts/llm_server.py` (resident workers ×N — R4/R6), `/Users/robert/code/genmlx/scripts/grpo_repl.cljs:147` (weight-bridge insertion point — R6 optional last).
